messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-project",
      "title": "Initialize project repository and structure",
      "description": "Create a new project folder, initialize git (optional) and npm, and create the basic directory structure (src, public, views, styles).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A repository folder exists with package.json, and directories src, public, views, and styles created and visible."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install required npm packages",
      "description": "Install Express and Tailwind build toolchain (tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer) plus any dev tooling (nodemon/build scripts) using npm or yarn.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "package.json lists express, tailwindcss, postcss, and autoprefixer (and optional dev tools), and node_modules contains installed packages."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind and build pipeline",
      "description": "Create tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js, add Tailwind directives to input CSS, and add npm scripts to build or watch Tailwind output into public CSS.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js exist and the build script produces a compiled CSS file in public (e.g., public/styles.css) containing Tailwind utilities."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Implement Express server to serve frontend",
      "description": "Write an Express app that serves static assets from public, renders or serves the main HTML file at '/', and includes development-friendly scripts (start/dev).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project",
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts without errors and visiting http://localhost:PORT returns the main HTML page and static assets load (CSS/JS)."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-hello",
      "title": "Create frontend HTML with Hello World text",
      "description": "Build the HTML page and base styling using Tailwind; include a visible \"hello world\" element with an identifiable id or class to be styled dynamically.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "An index.html (or template) served by Express displays 'hello world' styled with Tailwind and the compiled CSS is applied."
    },
    {
      "id": "add-client-interaction",
      "title": "Add UI controls and client JS for color modification",
      "description": "Implement a small frontend UI (color picker inputs, preset buttons, or dropdowns) and client-side JavaScript that updates the 'hello world' text color in real time.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "build-frontend-hello"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "User can change the 'hello world' text color via the UI and see immediate updates in the browser without reloading."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-document",
      "title": "Test functionality and add README",
      "description": "Manually test the app in a browser, verify static serving and color changes work across modern browsers, fix issues, and write a README with setup and usage instructions.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-express-server",
        "add-client-interaction"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README explains how to install, build, start the server, and use the color controls; tests confirm the app works locally and issues (if any) are noted or resolved."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down functional decomposition: create project skeleton, install/configure backend and styling pipeline, implement frontend Hello World page, add interactive UI for color modification, then test and document."
}
